data.title: Marvell rises after top executives buy shares of chipmaker
data.description:
Shares of Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) rose about 3% on Thursday after regulatory filings showed that several company executives bought the chipmaker's stock.

Chairman and CEO Matt Murphy bought 13,600 shares at $77 apiece. Following this, Murphy owns 268,637 shares of the company.

Chris Koopmans, president and chief operating officer, acquired 6,800 shares at $78.03 per share and now owns 104,825 shares of Marvell.

The company's CFO Willem Meintjes bought 3,400 shares at $78.03 apiece. Meintjes now has 132,159 shares in total.

Sandeep Bharathi, President of the Data Center Group at Marvell, acquired 3,400 shares at $78.03 per share and now owns 73,392 shares of Marvell.

data.title: xAI's Grok receives clearance for use across federal agencies; Musk sues OpenAI over trade secrets
data.description:
xAI, which is run by Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, has received approval for some of its Grok artificial intelligence models to be used by federal government employees, according to the U.S. General Services Administration.

Federal agencies can gain access to Grok 4 and Grok 4 Fast for $0.42 per organization for the next 18 months.

"Widespread access to advanced AI models is essential to building the efficient, accountable government that taxpayers deserve—and to fulfilling President Trump's promise that America will win the global AI race," said Federal Acquisition Service Commissioner Josh Gruenbaum.

"Thanks to President Trump and his administration, xAI's frontier AI is now unlocked for every federal agency empowering the U.S. Government to innovate faster and accomplish its mission more effectively than ever before," said Musk, xAI co-founder and CEO. "We look forward to continuing to work with President Trump and his team to rapidly deploy AI throughout the government for the benefit of the country."

The deal is even cheaper than other AI models that have received approval this summer to be used by the federal government. Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI is providing access to ChatGPT Enterprise to U.S. federal agencies at a nominal cost of $1 per agency for the next year. The GSA has also added AI services from Anthropic's Claude and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini.

xAI sues OpenAI

Musk's xAI has also filed a lawsuit against OpenAI. The civil suit alleges former xAI employees stole company secrets related to Grok and provided them to OpenAI.

"The desire to win the artificial intelligence race has driven OpenAI to cross the line of fair play," reads the lawsuit, which was filed in the U.S. District Court for Northern California on Wednesday, Sept. 24. "OpenAI violated California and federal law by inducing former xAI employees, including Xuechen Li, Jimmy Fraiture, and a senior finance executive, to steal and share xAI's trade secrets. By hook or by crook, OpenAI clearly will do anything when threatened by a better innovator, including plundering and misappropriating the technical advancements, source code, and business plans of xAI."

data.title: DMG Blockchain lands Buy rating from Canaccord
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity recently initiated coverage on DMG Blockchain Solutions (OTCQB:DMGGF) (TSXV:DMGI:CA) with a Buy rating, owing to the digital infrastructure company's "triple growth engine."

Analyst Tania Armstrong-Whitworth talked up DMG's (OTCQB:DMGGF) diversified, "balanced approach," with exposure to both bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining and software services.

"We believe this balanced approach positions DMGI as one of the more technically sophisticated and disciplined operators in the North American landscape," the analyst wrote in a note.

DMGGF shares, changing hands at $0.24 a share, dipped 6.6% in Thursday late morning trading.

data.title: Clean Energy Fuels to build second hydrogen station for Foothill Transit
data.description:
Clean Energy Fuels (NASDAQ:CLNE) said Thursday it was awarded a contract to design, build and maintain a second hydrogen fueling station for California's Foothill Transit, whose 357-bus fleet serves more than 11 million customers each year in Los Angeles County's San Gabriel and Pomona Valleys.

Clean Energy (NASDAQ:CLNE) said the $11.3 million project will be located at Foothill Transit's Arcadia bus yard, which serves as a fueling depot for the agency's renewable natural gas bus fleet; the station will support an initial order of 19 new hydrogen fuel cell buses.

The new Arcadia station, which will store liquid hydrogen and dispense it in gaseous form, is in the design stage with construction scheduled to begin in mid-2026.

The contract marks the continuation of a 20-year partnership between Clean Energy (CLNE) and Foothill Transit; the companies' first hydrogen station, commissioned in 2021 at Foothill's Pomona location, has been operational since June 2023 and supports 33 zero-emission hydrogen fuel cell buses.

data.title: PBMs reportedly eyeing changes to business conduct to avert regulatory scrutiny
data.description:
Pharmacy benefit managers, which function as intermediaries between pharmacies and health insurers, are working on a proposal to voluntarily adjust some of their business operations in a bid to avoid fresh regulatory scrutiny from the Trump administration, Bloomberg News reported.

The PBM trade group Pharmaceutical Care Management Association has prepared draft proposals to bring to the attention of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services ahead of potential new regulations against the industry, according to a document seen by Bloomberg News.

PCMA includes leading PBM operators such as CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (NYSE:CI), UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV), and Humana (HUM).

Among proposals cited in the report are plans to widen the availability of low-cost alternatives to biologics and increase payments to rural and independent pharmacies. A proposal to ensure that insured patients will not pay more than a pharmacy would charge someone without insurance is also under discussion.

To convince the CMS, PCMA is forwarding a range of ideas, including an argument to indicate that a well-publicized initiative will trigger favorable media coverage, according to the document.

The initiative comes as successive U.S. governments increasingly target PBMs for their role in driving up drug prices in the U.S. President Donald Trump issued an executive order in May calling on pharma companies to lower U.S. drug prices and pledging he will also “cut out” pharma middlemen from the sector.

data.title: Ticketmaster agrees to UK watchdog's terms after Oasis reunion tour complaints
data.description:
Live Nation-owned Ticketmaster (LVY) on Thursday agreed to commitments listed out by the UK's Competition and Markets Authority about the way it sells passes and advertises them after concertgoers who attended the Oasis' reunion tour complained about high ticket prices.

The regulator opened an investigation into Ticketmaster UK Ltd. and found the company did not tell fans waiting in lengthy queues that standing tickets were being sold at two different prices and that prices would jump as soon as the lower-priced tickets sold out.

CMA also found that Ticketmaster did not provide sufficient explanation to customers and sold some "platinum" tickets at almost two and a half times the price of "standard" tickets.

Concertgoers had complained the company used an algorithmic pricing model, or "dynamic pricing," to sell Oasis tickets, but the CMA's investigation did not find any evidence of such sales tactics.

Going forward, the UK watchdog has ordered the company to notify customers 24 hours in advance if a tiered pricing system is being used for an event, provide more information about ticket prices during online queues, not use any misleading ticket labels, and provide regular reports showing how it has complied with the commitments.

"We welcome the CMA’s confirmation there was no dynamic pricing, no unfair practices, and that we did not breach consumer law," Ticketmaster said.

data.title: Wall Street drops as jobless claims fall and Q2 GDP was revised higher
data.description:
Wall Street’s recent downward push continued on Thursday as the broader market averages observe selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

Falling the hardest has been the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which has dropped 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has found itself lower by 0.5% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is in the red by 0.3%. 

Across sectors, 10 of the 11 S&P groups are in negative trading territory, with Health Care suffering the most. At the other end, Energy has been the strongest performing segment on the session so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields are also on the move higher. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 4 basis points to 4.19% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.66%. At the same time the dollar index (DXY) tagged a 3-week high.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims dropped for the second week.

Q2 GDP was revised higher to +3.8% in the third estimate, intensifying the rebound.

Durable goods orders surprised higher in August, while the U.S. international goods trade deficit narrowed more than expected in August.

“Thursday's upward GDP revision for second quarter confirmed that the economy grew at a healthy clip, even as tariff uncertainty reached fever pitch during the quarter. The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

“Thursday's GDP strength likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected path of rate cuts, since the data is backward looking and was already well-known. The Fed wants to lower rates to keep the labor market in check and is able to do so at this time since inflation has remained under control, even though it's still above the Fed's target,” Stanley added.

Furthermore, the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.

data.title: Palo Alto Networks gets HSR early termination for CyberArk acquisition
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) has received an early termination notice for its planned $25 billion acquisition of CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR). 

The early termination notice was disclosed on the Federal Trade Commission's website on Thursday. 

Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) agreed to acquire identity security firm CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR) for an equity value of about $25 billion in late July.

data.title: Centrus Energy launches multibillion dollar expansion of Ohio uranium plant
data.description:
Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +9.9% in Thursday's trading after detailing plans for a multibillion dollar expansion of its uranium enrichment facility in Piketon, Ohio, boosting production of low enriched uranium and high assay low enriched uranium production.

Subject to being selected for funding by the U.S. Department of Energy, Centrus (NYSE:LEU) said its expansion plans call for a multibillion dollar public and private investment to add thousands of additional centrifuges at the American Centrifuge Plant, delivering large-scale production of both LEU and HALEU.

The project also is expected to support hundreds of jobs at Centrus' (LEU) centrifuge manufacturing plant in Tennessee and throughout its domestic manufacturing supply chain, the company said.

In anticipation of the expansion, Centrus (LEU) said it has raised more than $1.2 billion in a pair of convertible note transactions, has secured more than $2 billion in contingent purchase commitments from utility customers, and recently announced a collaboration with Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power and POSCO International for a potential investment in the project.

data.title: NORAD jets intercept Russian aircraft near Alaska
data.description:
The North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) said Thursday it scrambled fighter jets a day earlier to track four Russian military planes operating near Alaska.

According to the command, two Tu-95 bombers and two Su-35 fighters were detected in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone on Wednesday. In response, NORAD dispatched an E-3 airborne warning plane, four F-16 fighters, and four KC-135 refueling aircraft.

The Russian planes stayed in international airspace and did not cross into U.S. or Canadian territory, NORAD said. The Alaskan identification zone, which covers international airspace, requires all aircraft to be readily identifiable for security reasons.
